---
title: "1. Introduction"
subtitle: "Geospatial Data Analysis"
author: "Prof. Dr. Halldór Janetzko"
header-logo: "_extensions/halldorjanetzko/hslu-reveal-theme-de/logos/hslu-logo.svg"
title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-image: "figures/cartography_teaser.png"
    data-background-size: contain
    data-background-opacity: "0.8"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, brand, ./_extensions/halldorjanetzko/hslu-reveal-theme-de/hslu_theme.scss, local.scss ]
    chalkboard: false
    incremental: false
    slide-number: "c"
    hide-from-titleSlide: "all"
    embed-resources: true
    subtitle-as-header: true
    footer: "{{< meta title >}}"
    sc-sb-title: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    template-partials: 
      - toc-slide.html
    include-after-body: 
      - toc-add.html
      - clean_title_page.html
filters: 
  - reveal-header
slide-level: 3
---

## Today's menu {.unnumbered .unlisted}

```{=html}
<div id='toc'></div>
```


## Why Quarto?

::: {.incremental}
- One source can target slides, HTML, PDF, Word, and more
- Markdown stays readable and version-control friendly
- Code, figures, tables, and narrative live in the same document
- Reproducible rendering reduces copy-paste workflows
- Cross-references, citations, and extensions are built in
- Other than in beamer, you can embed videos as HTML5 videos
:::

---

## Quarto In Practice

::: {.incremental}
- Good fit for teaching material, reports, and technical presentations
- Works well with R, Python, Julia, and Observable content
- Reveal.js output supports interactive slides and custom themes
- Project structure stays simple: content, assets, and configuration
- Publishing and export can be automated in local scripts or CI
:::

---

### Exporting Slides To PDF

Quarto can render reveal.js slides directly, but browser-based slide decks are also commonly exported with **DeckTape**.

::: {.incremental}
- DeckTape captures HTML slide decks and prints them to PDF
- There is also a docker container available for DeckTape
- Useful when you want a shareable PDF version of a reveal.js presentation
- The project is available on GitHub: <https://github.com/astefanutti/decktape>
- Typical workflow: render the slides with Quarto, then run DeckTape on the generated HTML
:::

---

```bash
quarto render presentation.qmd
decktape reveal presentation.html presentation.pdf
```

---

### Docker Container for DeckTape

Dockerfile.decktape-fonts

#### 

```Dockerfile
FROM astefanutti/decktape:latest

USER root

# Microsoft Core Fonts (inkl. Verdana) + Cache bauen
RUN apk add --no-cache fontconfig cabextract wget msttcorefonts-installer \
  && update-ms-fonts \
  && fc-cache -f

USER node
```

### Docker Compose

```yaml
decktape:
    build:
      context: /home/iajanetz/docker-compose-home
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.decktape-fonts
    container_name: decktape
    user: "1000:1000"
    volumes:
      - /home/iajanetz:/home/iajanetz:ro
      - /home/iajanetz/quarto_decktape_output:/output:rw
    working_dir: /home/iajanetz
``` 

### Script to run DeckTape in Docker

``` bash
#!/usr/bin/env zsh
set -euo pipefail

# --- Config (adjust if needed) ---
THEME_REPO="halldorjanetzko/hslu-reveal-theme-de"
DOCKER_COMPOSE_HOME="/home/iajanetz/docker-compose-home"
DECKTAPE_SERVICE="decktape"
HOST_OUTPUT_DIR="/home/iajanetz/quarto_decktape_output"
# --------------------------------

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage:
  quarto-decktape.zsh <path-to-presentation.qmd | path-to-folder>

Examples:
  quarto-decktape.zsh 01_introduction/presentation.qmd
  quarto-decktape.zsh 01_introduction

What it does:
  1) quarto update <theme repo>
  2) quarto render presentation.qmd
  3) docker compose run decktape reveal <abs html path> /output/<foldername>.pdf
EOF
}

if [[ "${1:-}" == "" || "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
  usage
  exit 0
fi

input="$1"

# Resolve input to a .qmd file and its directory
if [[ -d "$input" ]]; then
  qmd="$input/presentation.qmd"
else
  qmd="$input"
fi

if [[ ! -f "$qmd" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Could not find presentation.qmd at: $qmd" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Absolute paths
qmd_abs="$(python3 -c 'import os,sys; print(os.path.realpath(sys.argv[1]))' "$qmd")"
qmd_dir="${qmd_abs:h}"
folder_name="${qmd_dir:t}"

html_abs="${qmd_dir}/presentation.html"

echo "==> Target QMD:  $qmd_abs"
echo "==> Folder name: $folder_name"

# 1) Update reveal theme
echo "==> (1/3) quarto update ${THEME_REPO}"
cd "$qmd_dir"
quarto update "$THEME_REPO"

# 2) Render HTML
echo "==> (2/3) quarto render presentation.qmd"
quarto render "presentation.qmd"

if [[ ! -f "$html_abs" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Expected HTML not found at: $html_abs" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# 3) Run decktape via docker compose
echo "==> (3/3) docker compose run decktape"
cd "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_HOME"

# Output path INSIDE the container (host mapping depends on your compose setup)
out_pdf="/output/${folder_name}.pdf"

echo "==> Rendering PDF to: ${out_pdf}"
docker compose run --rm "$DECKTAPE_SERVICE" reveal "$html_abs" "$out_pdf"

echo "✅ Done. Decktape output: $out_pdf"

if [[ -d "$HOST_OUTPUT_DIR" ]]; then
  if command -v nautilus >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    nautilus --new-window "$HOST_OUTPUT_DIR" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
  else
    # Fallback: open with default file manager
    xdg-open "$HOST_OUTPUT_DIR" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
  fi
else
  echo "⚠️ Output directory not found on host: $HOST_OUTPUT_DIR" >&2
fi
```
